data.title: Benign consumer inflation report cements Fed interest rate cut odds
data.description:
The final U.S. consumer inflation report of the year on Wednesday cemented expectations for another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve's monetary policy committee next week.

The headline and core consumer price index (CPI) for November came in-line with consensus estimates, according to data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. While the readings were benign, the headline CPI did accelerate on a M/M basis to its highest level since April.

As per the CME FedWatch tool, the odds of a 25 basis point rate cut by the Fed next week inched up to nearly 95% after the CPI report, from 89% a day ago and 78% a week ago.

"We expect the FOMC will reduce the federal funds rate by 25 bps at the conclusion of its upcoming meeting on December 18 while simultaneously emphasizing that future rate cuts will be slower-going and dependent on incoming data," Wells Fargo's Jay Bryson said.

"Data over the inter-meeting period suggest ongoing resilience within the U.S. economy. The labor market cooling remains contained while the last few inflation readings show a stubborn pace of price growth that remains about a percentage point above the central bank's 2% target," Bryson added.

See here for views on the CPI data from various experts.

For investors looking to track the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

More on the markets

  • November CPI: General Disinflation Stalls But Services And Shelter Cool
  • November CPI: Second Consecutive Inflation Increase, Not Cause For Concern
  • S&P 500: A Year-End Record, A Challenging 2025, And A 'Lost Decade'
  • ‘Stock pickers’ will be the best investment strategy in the near-term – SG 2025 Outlook
  • Nasdaq hits 20K points in historic first after inflation data cements Fed rate-cut bets

data.title: ‘Stock pickers’ will be the best investment strategy in the near-term – SG 2025 Outlook
data.description:
The backdrop for “stock pickers” is the best it has been in 25 years with correlation of stock performance at all-time lows, according to Societe Generale in their 2025 Outlook report.

The S&P 500 (SP500) is up 6% since the election, “in line with our projection that the most bullish outcome for US stocks would be a ‘red sweep,’” said Manish Kabra, head of U.S. equity strategy and multi-asset strategist.

Societe Generale analysts expect the S&P 500 (SP500) to perform in line with the profit cycle, and their EPS growth forecast is at 11%.

“We expect that ‘what to buy’ will remain one of the most important elements, and we will take our cues, both positive and negative, from the potential Republican policies and the profit-cycle,” said Kabra.

Three “big positives” expected under the Trump administration are the potential for lower taxes, less regulation and lower oil prices.

Lower taxes

“We estimate that roughly half of the S&P 500 (SP500) is impacted by the manufacturing cycle but that almost 60% of the manufacturing base is outside the U.S.,” said Kabra. “The first-order impact of tax reform is likely to be a positive 2-3% lift to EPS.”

Less regulation

“The greatest impact of Trump’s deregulatory policy stance could be in the financials’ sector (XLF), where a raft of regulations could be watered down,” said Kabra. “Financials remain our key overweight, and we expect the current backdrop to be conducive to further outperformance of the higher-beta part of the sector.”

Lower oil prices

“If the tax benefits for domestic manufacturing are extended to the oil and gas (XOP) companies, it may lead to higher rig counts,” he said. “Hence, if we were to position within the oil sector (OIH), we would suggest long oil capex over the energy sector (XLE).”

On the other hand, the potential for higher tariffs and a related trade war, the continued uncertainty on inflation and Federal Reserve rate cuts, and fiscal overspending “would be likely to raise the cost of funding for the U.S. government and ultimately for U.S. corporates too,” said Kabra.

data.title: TUI AG reports Q4 results
data.description:
• TUI AG press release (OTCPK:TUIFF): Q4 Underlying EPS of €1.70
  • Revenue of €9.43B, vs. €8.47B a year ago
  • FY25 Guidance
  • Revenue to increase by 5%-10% yoy
  • Underlying EBIT to increase by 7%-10% yoy driven in particular by expectations for Summer 2025

data.title: Relay stock climbs 6% on positive breast cancer data for RLY-2608
data.description:
• Shares of Relay Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RLAY) were up 6% in midday trading Wednesday after the company reported positive updated interim data from an early-stage clinical trial for its drug RLY-2608 in the treatment of breast cancer.
  • The data showed a median progression-free survival of 11.4 months in second-line patients with PI3Kα-mutated, HR+, HER2- locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer who received RLY-2608 600mg twice daily plus fulvestrant, according to a statement.
  • The data is being presented Wednesday at the San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium.
  • Relay said it had cash and equivalents of around $840M at the end of Q3.

data.title: Hilton, Grubhub Onsite expand partnership
data.description:
• Grubhub Onsite and Hilton (NYSE:HLT) are expanding their partnership by bringing on-demand food, grocery, and convenience item delivery to over 2,600 hotels across the Hampton by Hilton, Tru by Hilton, and Spark by Hilton brands.
  • This expansion means more guests can now enjoy seamless access to Grubhub's extensive Marketplace of more than 375,000 merchants by simply scanning a QR code, offering the ultimate convenience during their stay.
  • Guests can also enjoy one free month of Grubhub+ for perks like $0 delivery fees and lower service fees on eligible orders, and 5% credit back on pickup orders, making it even easier for guests to get the items they need.
  • This expanded partnership complements Hilton's existing offerings by providing guests with additional flexibility to meet their unique needs and cravings.
  • "Expanding our partnership with Hilton is a testament to our successful alliance and the strong demand for our service among guests seeking convenient, affordable delivery," said Rob DelaCruz, vice president and general manager of Grubhub Onsite.

data.title: Industria de Diseno Textil Inditex reports 9M results
data.description:
• Industria de Diseno Textil Inditex press release (OTCPK:IDEXY): 9M net income of €4.4 billion.
  • Sales grew 7.1% to reach €27.4 billion.

data.title: OpenAI gives further updates on ChatGPT integration with Siri amid rollout
data.description:
• As part of its "12 days of OpenAI" event, the Sam Altman-led company gave further details on its ChatGPT integration into Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) products.
  • ChatGPT can be accessed via the operating system (either anonymously or with an account), in the Settings menu, OpenAI said during its event. It can also be accessed directly via the Siri voice assistant.
  • It also showed off how ChatGPT is integrated into Apple's Visual Intelligence, which lets iPhone users learn about the world around them, akin to Google Lens.
  • Apple officially rolled out iOS 18.2 — along with new versions of macOS and iPadOS — this morning, which integrates ChatGPT into its various operating systems. OpenAI is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and recently raised $6.6B in funding that values the company at $157B.
  • The news comes as OpenAI has made several announcements during its "12 days of OpenAI" event. It kicked off the event announcing its $200 per month version of ChatGPT Pro, while Day 2 highlighted its Reinforcement Fine-Tuning Research Program. Days 3 and 4 saw the launch of its AI video generator Sora and its AI-powered writing and coding tool Canvas, respectively.

Seeking Alpha's Brandon Evans contributed to this story.

data.title: Semi stocks rise helps propel Nasdaq above 20,000 points
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks moved higher by early afternoon trading on Wednesday, helping propel the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) above 20,000 points.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) was up 2.5% while AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) edged up nearly 1%. It was reported earlier today that both companies have invested in semiconductor startup Ayar Labs.

Meanwhile, Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) led the charge as it more than recovered from Tuesday's decline as it climbed 5%. It was revealed today that Broadcom is working with Apple (AAPL) to design an in-house chip specifically for artificial intelligence applications. Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM) will reportedly produce the chip, which is expected for mass production by 2026. TSM inched up 1%.

Broadcom is slated to release its fourth quarter financial results post-market on Thursday.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) increased nearly 3% by afternoon trading. The Idaho-based memory chipmaker announced yesterday it is on track to receive more than $6B in direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act.

Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) had gained 4.5%. The company announced late on Wednesday it had made a breakthrough in custom high-bandwidth memory used in artificial intelligence processors.

Other semi stocks moving up by Wednesday afternoon included Texas Instruments (TXN) +0.7%, Analog Devices (ADI) +0.6%, NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) +0.7%, Qualcomm (QCOM) +1.5%, and Applied Materials (AMAT) +1.6%.

Intel (INTC) was one of the few semi stocks trending lower during the day, as it was down about a quarter of a percent.

Many U.S. stocks across the board seemed to benefit from an in-line consumer inflation report that solidified expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week.

data.title: US Steel seesaws on report Biden administration split over Nippon Steel deal
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) shares seesawed after a report that the Biden administration is split over a decision on the steelmaker's planned $14 billion sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY), though Joe Biden still plans to block it. US Steel rose ~1%.

The Pentagon, US Treasury and state department have all decided that the US Steel deal doesn't pose a national security risk, according to a Financial Times report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

US trade representative Katherine Tai is opposed to the combination and the US commerce and energy departments also have some concerns, according to the report.

The FT item comes after US Steel (X) shares fell almost 10% on Tuesday after Bloomberg reported that Biden will formally block the deal once the Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.S. gives him the regulator's recommendation.

Deputy cabinet secretaries and top officials from CFIUS agencies will meet Wednesday to discuss the transaction before CFIUS makes a formal recommendation, the FT reported.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) and US Steel are likely to pursue litigation if Biden moves with a block of the transaction, according to the Bloomberg report.

Last month, it was reported that Japan's Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba sent a letter to Biden asking him to approve the Nippon Steel's (OTCPK:NPSCY) deal to avoid marring recent efforts to strengthen ties between the countries.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher closes stock offering
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) announced the closing of its $8.5B public offering of 30.36M shares of common stock, priced at $280.00 per share.
  • The insurance broker had announced the pricing of the stock offering yesterday.
  • Source: Press Release
